name: AWS Certified Machine Learning Engineer – Associate

domains.name: Domain 1: Data Preparation for Machine Learning
Domain 3: Deployment and Orchestration of ML Workflows
Domain 4: ML Solution Monitoring, Maintenance, and Security

questions.text: A regulated workload requires the organization to visually prepare tabular data for ML without code. Which solution is BEST? visually prepare tabular data for ML without code.
questions.explanation: Data Wrangler imports, transforms, and exports ML data.

questions.text: While troubleshooting latency, engineers determine they must run ETL on large datasets in a data lake. Which action is appropriate? run ETL on large datasets in a data lake.
questions.explanation: Glue provides serverless Spark ETL.

questions.text: A hybrid environment must support a goal to label images for object detection without changing application code. What should you implement? label images for object detection.
questions.explanation: Ground Truth manages human labeling workflows.

questions.text: During peak traffic, the platform team must detect schema and statistics on S3 data. What should they do FIRST? detect schema and statistics on S3 data.

options.text: AWS Glue Data Catalog and crawlers

questions.explanation: The Data Catalog stores table metadata.

questions.text: After a design review, leadership requires the team to handle class imbalance in training data. Which approach is MOST reliable? handle class imbalance in training data.

options.text: Oversampling, weights, or SMOTE techniques in SageMaker

questions.explanation: Imbalanced data requires sampling or weighted loss.

questions.text: The DevOps team is automating releases and must split data into train/validation/test without leakage. Which design fits best? split data into train/validation/test without leakage.

options.text: SageMaker Processing or sklearn split with stratification

questions.explanation: Proper splits prevent optimistic metrics.

questions.text: Operations reports a need to store feature values for online inference. What is the MOST operationally efficient solution? store feature values for online inference.
questions.explanation: Feature Store serves consistent features in training and inference.

questions.text: After a design review, leadership requires the team to anonymize PII before model training. Which approach is MOST reliable? anonymize PII before model training.
questions.explanation: Remove or tokenize sensitive attributes early.

questions.text: During peak traffic, the platform team must train models with managed infrastructure and spot savings. What should they do FIRST? train models with managed infrastructure and spot savings.
questions.explanation: Training jobs scale on ML instances with spot support.

questions.text: A hybrid environment must support a goal to tune hyperparameters automatically without changing application code. What should you implement? tune hyperparameters automatically.
questions.explanation: Automatic Model Tuning searches hyperparameter space.

questions.text: A company needs to track experiments and parameters. Which option meets the requirement with the LEAST implementation effort? track experiments and parameters.
questions.explanation: Experiment tracking compares runs reproducibly.

questions.text: A regulated workload requires the organization to use built-in algorithms for common ML tasks. Which solution is BEST? use built-in algorithms for common ML tasks.
questions.explanation: Built-ins include XGBoost, Linear Learner, and more.

questions.text: A hybrid environment must support a goal to bring custom Docker container for training without changing application code. What should you implement? bring custom Docker container for training.

options.text: Arbitrary root on public hub only

questions.explanation: Custom containers package proprietary dependencies.

questions.text: A company needs to distributed training across multiple GPUs. Which option meets the requirement with the LEAST implementation effort? distributed training across multiple GPUs.
questions.explanation: Distributed libraries scale deep learning jobs.

questions.text: After a design review, leadership requires the team to version datasets and models together. Which approach is MOST reliable? version datasets and models together.

options.text: SageMaker Model Registry with dataset snapshots in S3

questions.explanation: Versioning supports rollback and audit.

questions.text: After a design review, leadership requires the team to debug training with real-time metrics. Which approach is MOST reliable? debug training with real-time metrics.
questions.explanation: Debugger detects issues like vanishing gradients.

questions.text: Operations reports a need to deploy real-time inference behind an endpoint. What is the MOST operationally efficient solution? deploy real-time inference behind an endpoint.
questions.explanation: Endpoints serve low-latency predictions.

questions.text: The DevOps team is automating releases and must run asynchronous inference for large payloads. Which design fits best? run asynchronous inference for large payloads.
questions.explanation: Async inference queues requests and returns results later.

questions.text: Operations reports a need to deploy multiple models behind one endpoint. What is the MOST operationally efficient solution? deploy multiple models behind one endpoint.

options.text: One endpoint per tiny model always costly

questions.explanation: Multi-model endpoints share infrastructure.

questions.text: A hybrid environment must support a goal to orchestrate ML CI/CD steps without changing application code. What should you implement? orchestrate ML CI/CD steps.
questions.explanation: Pipelines define reproducible ML workflows.

questions.text: For cost and security, the team must use infrastructure as code for SageMaker resources. What is the recommended approach? use infrastructure as code for SageMaker resources.
questions.explanation: IaC codifies ML infrastructure.

questions.text: During peak traffic, the platform team must A/B test model variants in production. What should they do FIRST? A/B test model variants in production.

options.text: SageMaker production variants with traffic weights

questions.explanation: Traffic splitting compares model performance safely.

questions.text: Operations reports a need to containerize inference for Kubernetes. What is the MOST operationally efficient solution? containerize inference for Kubernetes.

options.text: SageMaker Inference Recommender or EKS hosting

questions.explanation: EKS can host containers built for SageMaker.

questions.text: An audit found gaps: the organization must schedule batch scoring nightly. Which option addresses this? schedule batch scoring nightly.

options.text: SageMaker Batch Transform or scheduled pipelines

questions.explanation: Batch Transform efficiently scores large datasets.

questions.text: For cost and security, the team must detect data drift in production features. What is the recommended approach? detect data drift in production features.
questions.explanation: Model Monitor compares live data to baselines.

questions.text: A regulated workload requires the organization to explain individual predictions to stakeholders. Which solution is BEST? explain individual predictions to stakeholders.
questions.explanation: Clarify provides SHAP and other explainability.

questions.text: The DevOps team is automating releases and must encrypt training volumes and model artifacts. Which design fits best? encrypt training volumes and model artifacts.

options.text: KMS encryption on EBS and S3 for SageMaker

questions.explanation: Encryption protects ML intellectual property.

questions.text: While troubleshooting latency, engineers determine they must restrict notebook internet access for compliance. Which action is appropriate? restrict notebook internet access for compliance.
questions.explanation: VPC-only Studio limits data exfiltration.

questions.text: A company needs to capture inference payloads for audit (where allowed). Which option meets the requirement with the LEAST implementation effort? capture inference payloads for audit (where allowed).
questions.explanation: Data capture records inputs/outputs per policy.

questions.text: A hybrid environment must support a goal to automatically roll back bad deployments without changing application code. What should you implement? automatically roll back bad deployments.

options.text: CloudWatch alarms + CodeDeploy or pipeline approvals

questions.explanation: Automated rollback limits incident blast radius.

questions.text: For cost and security, the team must scan containers for CVEs before deploy. What is the recommended approach? scan containers for CVEs before deploy.
questions.explanation: Inspector scans images in ECR.

questions.text: An audit found gaps: the organization must audit who invoked endpoints. Which option addresses this? audit who invoked endpoints.
questions.explanation: Logging supports security investigations.

questions.text: Which TWO official AWS resources help you prepare for AWS Certified Machine Learning Engineer – Associate? (Select TWO.)
questions.explanation: Use official AWS exam guides, Skill Builder, and AWS documentation—not real exam content.

questions.text: Which TWO practices align with the AWS Well-Architected Framework? (Select TWO.)

options.text: Design for failure and automate recovery
Use single-AZ for all production data stores

questions.explanation: Reliability and operational excellence emphasize automation, testing, and learning from failure.

questions.text: Which are AWS services used for encryption or key management? (Select TWO.)
questions.explanation: KMS manages keys; Secrets Manager stores and rotates secrets.
